rumor: [Realm] Smoke hangs over the peaks — a wyrm has woken beneath Emberpeak, and its brood burns the farms of Emberfell. Reeve Halden offers good coin to any blade who will end the raids.

areas.name: Emberfell Village
areas.legend: A hard little mountain village of drystone and slate, its terraced fields blackened where the wyrm's brood have passed. Reeve Halden musters what defenders he can.

areas.name: The Charred Vale
areas.legend: A high scorched valley strewn with cracked bone and the melted iron of lost travellers — the wyrm's hunting ground. A single great scale glints among the cinders.

areas.name: Yorwin's Refuge
areas.legend: A hermit's turf-roofed hut hidden in the pines below the peaks, its walls papered with charts of wing and flame. Here the exiled dragon-scholar Yorwin keeps his long vigil.

areas.name: The Wyrm's Roost
areas.legend: A cave mouth ringed with the slag of a hundred hoards, breathing heat into the cold mountain air. Cindermaw the wyrm coils here upon its bright-won plunder.

reeve_halden.name: Reeve Halden
reeve_halden.description: A broad, grey-bearded man with a soldier's bearing gone to farming, the reeve of Emberfell. He has buried three neighbours this season and means to bury no more.
goals: End the wyrm's raids on my village
Keep my people from fleeing the mountain

shepherd_bryn.name: Shepherd Bryn
shepherd_bryn.description: A young shepherd, soot-streaked and hollow-eyed, who watched a whelp carry off half his flock and can point you to where the burning began.
goals: Warn travellers off the high pastures

loremaster_yorwin.name: Loremaster Yorwin
loremaster_yorwin.description: An exiled dragon-scholar, lean and hawk-eyed, who spent a lifetime cataloguing wyrms until his guild named him a heretic. He alone knows where Cindermaw's scales never healed.
goals: Prove the guild wrong about the great wyrms
See a wyrm's weakness put to the test
Finish my life's catalogue before I die

metadata.secret: beneath the jaw, where the old wound never scaled

resolved: With Cindermaw dead and its brood scattered, the terraced fields of Emberfell are safe to sow again and the high roads reopen.
